## [03:01 - 06:02]

only seven of them show good potential. I'll move those seven into a single ad set in website conversion, because then I want to bundle those together. Now, that's my preferred way of doing it. The other way is you actually just move them in that same way into separate ad sets in website conversion. But the problem is that it's not just about the size of the audience, because there may be a big overlap. But when you group them together, Facebook will optimize between those interests as well and pull out the people that are more likely to buy. And so when I'm spending bigger budgets, not only do I want bigger audiences, but if I've got multiple interests, and I know that the majority of them work, I also want them in the same group so that I can kind of bundle my spend into that one ad set as well.
